documents.title: Retail Lease — Patagonia Outdoor Co. at Blue Ridge Town Center

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 2,800 rentable square feet, the sum of $38.50 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $8,983.33 in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the Term, without deduction, setoff, prior notice, or demand. Base Rent shall be subject to annual escalation as provided in Section 4.2.

clauses.heading: Rent Escalation
clauses.text: Commencing on the first anniversary of the Rent Commencement Date and on each anniversary thereafter during the Term, Base Rent shall increase by three percent (3%) over the Base Rent payable during the immediately preceding Lease Year. Such increases shall be cumulative and compounding. By illustration, Base Rent in Lease Year 2 shall be $39.655 per rentable square foot per annum, and in Lease Year 3 shall be $40.845 per rentable square foot per annum. In no event shall any annual adjustment result in a decrease in Base Rent.

clauses.heading: Insurance Requirements
clauses.text: Tenant shall, at its sole cost and expense, maintain throughout the Term: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 in the aggregate; (b) property insurance covering Tenant's personal property and trade fixtures on an all-risk basis at full replacement cost; and (c) workers' compensation insurance as required by applicable North Carolina law. All policies shall name Landlord and any holder of a mortgage or deed of trust on the Property as additional insureds. Tenant shall deliver certificates of insurance to Landlord no later than the Commencement Date and within ten (10) days following each annual renewal.

documents.title: First Amendment to Retail Lease — Patagonia Outdoor Co.

clauses.heading: Expansion of Premises
clauses.text: Effective October 1, 2022, the Premises are hereby expanded to include Suite 104-B, consisting of approximately 320 additional rentable square feet (the 'Expansion Space'), for a total of approximately 3,120 rentable square feet. Base Rent attributable to the Expansion Space shall be $38.50 per rentable square foot per annum, subject to the same annual escalation schedule set forth in Section 4.2 of the Lease, applied to the consolidated Premises from the Expansion Date forward.

clauses.heading: Permitted Use — Worn Wear Program
clauses.text: Section 6.1 of the Lease is hereby amended to permit Tenant to operate a limited repair and resale program for used outdoor apparel and equipment within the Expansion Space (the 'Worn Wear Program'), in addition to Tenant's existing first-line retail operations. Tenant shall not use the Expansion Space in any manner that generates pedestrian traffic or operational impacts materially inconsistent with the standards of a first-class lifestyle retail center, as reasonably determined by Landlord.

documents.title: Retail Lease — Warby Parker LLC at Westfield Commons

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 1,600 rentable square feet, the sum of $44.00 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $5,866.67 in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the Term. Base Rent is subject to adjustment as provided in Section 4.2 and shall be payable without offset, deduction, or prior demand.

clauses.heading: Annual Rent Escalation
clauses.text: On the first day of each Lease Year following Lease Year 1, Base Rent shall increase by two and one-half percent (2.5%) over the Base Rent in effect for the immediately preceding Lease Year. Such adjustments are cumulative and compounding. No adjustment shall cause Base Rent to decrease below the rate in effect for the preceding Lease Year.

clauses.heading: Insurance
clauses.text: Tenant shall procure and maintain at Tenant's expense throughout the Term: (a) commercial general liability insurance in an amount not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 aggregate; (b) all-risk property insurance on Tenant's trade fixtures, furniture, and leasehold improvements at replacement cost value; and (c) workers' compensation coverage as required under Colorado law. Each policy shall name Landlord, Landlord's property manager, and Landlord's lender as additional insureds and shall provide thirty (30) days' notice to Landlord prior to cancellation.

documents.title: Retail Lease — SoulCycle Inc. at Lakefront Plaza

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 4,200 rentable square feet, the sum of $52.00 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $18,200.00 in advance on the first day of each calendar month. Base Rent shall be subject to CPI adjustment as set forth in Section 4.2. All payments shall be made without prior notice, demand, or offset.

clauses.heading: CPI Rent Adjustment
clauses.text: Base Rent shall be adjusted annually on each anniversary of the Rent Commencement Date by a percentage equal to the annual change in the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers, Chicago-Naperville-Elgin metropolitan area (CPI-U), as published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics for the twelve (12) month period ending sixty (60) days prior to the applicable adjustment date; provided, however, that in no event shall any single annual adjustment exceed five percent (5%) or be less than one and one-half percent (1.5%), regardless of the actual CPI change.

clauses.heading: Required Insurance
clauses.text: Throughout the Term, Tenant shall carry and maintain at Tenant's sole expense: (a) commercial general liability insurance with per-occurrence limits of not less than $2,000,000 and annual aggregate limits of not less than $4,000,000, specifically covering fitness studio operations including bodily injury to class participants; (b) property insurance on Tenant's personal property and studio equipment at full replacement cost on an all-risk basis; and (c) workers' compensation and employer's liability insurance as required by the Illinois Workers' Compensation Act. Landlord, Landlord's managing agent, and any recorded mortgagee shall be named as additional insureds on all liability policies.

clauses.heading: Renewal Option
clauses.text: Provided Tenant is not in default beyond applicable notice and cure periods, Tenant shall have one (1) option to renew this Lease for an additional period of five (5) years (the 'Renewal Term') upon no less than twelve (12) months' prior written notice to Landlord. Base Rent during the Renewal Term shall be the greater of (i) the then-current Base Rent in effect immediately preceding the Renewal Term, or (ii) ninety-five percent (95%) of the then-prevailing fair market rental rate for comparable fitness studio space in the Chicago CBD submarket, as mutually agreed by the parties or, failing agreement, determined by a single MAI-certified appraiser mutually selected within thirty (30) days of Landlord's receipt of Tenant's renewal notice.

documents.title: Reciprocal Easement Agreement — Lakefront Plaza

clauses.heading: Common Area Maintenance Obligations
clauses.text: Each party shall maintain its respective portion of the Shopping Center in a clean, safe, and first-class condition consistent with comparable retail developments in the Chicago metropolitan area. Common maintenance costs for shared infrastructure — including perimeter landscaping, exterior lighting, and stormwater management systems — shall be allocated sixty percent (60%) to the anchor parcel owner and forty percent (40%) to the in-line retail parcels, based on respective leasable square footage, subject to annual reconciliation.

clauses.heading: Cross-Access and Parking
clauses.text: Each party hereby grants to the other party, and to their respective tenants, customers, and invitees, a perpetual non-exclusive easement for pedestrian and vehicular access, ingress, and egress over the Common Parking Area and drive aisles depicted on Exhibit B hereto. Neither party shall erect barriers, fences, or structures that materially impair such cross-access without the prior written consent of the other party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed.

clauses.heading: Signage Coordination
clauses.text: All exterior signage on the pylon sign and monument signage structures identified on Exhibit C shall be subject to a signage plan mutually approved by both parties, acting reasonably and in good faith. Neither party shall install or modify pylon sign panels without providing the other party at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice and obtaining any required governmental approvals. Sign panel priority shall be allocated: Tier 1 — anchor tenant; Tier 2 — in-line tenants greater than 5,000 rentable square feet; Tier 3 — all remaining in-line tenants, on a first-come, first-served basis.

documents.title: Retail Lease — Lululemon Athletica Inc. at Harbor Point Marketplace

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 2,400 rentable square feet, the sum per rentable square foot per annum set forth in Section 4.2, payable in equal monthly installments in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the applicable Lease Year period, without deduction or offset. Base Rent for the initial Lease Year shall be $56.00 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in monthly installments of $11,200.00.

clauses.heading: Stepped Rent Schedule
clauses.text: Notwithstanding Section 4.1, Base Rent shall escalate pursuant to the following schedule: (i) Lease Years 1 through 3 (September 1, 2019 – August 31, 2022): $56.00 per rentable square foot per annum ($11,200.00 per month); (ii) Lease Years 4 through 6 (September 1, 2022 – August 31, 2025): $61.50 per rentable square foot per annum ($12,300.00 per month); (iii) Lease Years 7 through 8 (September 1, 2025 – August 31, 2027): $67.00 per rentable square foot per annum ($13,400.00 per month). Each step increase shall be self-effectuating on the applicable date without further notice or amendment.

clauses.heading: Tenant's Insurance Obligations
clauses.text: Tenant shall procure and maintain, at Tenant's sole cost, throughout the Term: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 aggregate; (b) product liability insurance of not less than $5,000,000 per occurrence, given Tenant's retail apparel operations; (c) property insurance at replacement cost covering all Tenant's property and leasehold improvements; and (d) workers' compensation as required by Washington State law. All policies shall name Landlord, Landlord's managing agent, and Landlord's lender as additional insureds. Certificates shall be delivered to Landlord prior to the Commencement Date.

clauses.heading: Renewal Options
clauses.text: Provided Tenant is not in monetary or material non-monetary default beyond applicable cure periods, Tenant shall have two (2) consecutive options to renew this Lease for five (5) years each. Each option must be exercised by written notice delivered to Landlord no later than twelve (12) months prior to expiration of the then-current Term. Base Rent during each Renewal Term shall be reset to ninety-five percent (95%) of the then-prevailing fair market rental value for comparable ground-floor retail space in the South Lake Union / Belltown corridor, as mutually agreed or determined by binding appraisal.

documents.title: Retail Lease — Restore Hyper Wellness Inc. at Meridian Crossing

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 3,500 rentable square feet, the fixed sum of $29.00 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $8,458.33 in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the Term. Notwithstanding any other provision hereof, Base Rent shall remain fixed and shall not be subject to increase during the initial Term, except as expressly provided with respect to Operating Expense Adjustments in Section 8.3.

clauses.heading: Co-Tenancy Condition
clauses.text: Tenant's obligation to pay Base Rent at the full rate specified in Section 4.1 is conditioned upon Whole Foods Market operating a fully open and continuously operating retail grocery store of no less than 30,000 square feet at the Shopping Center (the 'Co-Tenancy Condition'). In the event the Co-Tenancy Condition is not satisfied for ninety (90) or more consecutive days (excluding closures for remodeling not to exceed one hundred twenty (120) days), Tenant shall have the right, upon thirty (30) days' written notice to Landlord, to pay Substitute Rent equal to six percent (6%) of Tenant's monthly Gross Sales in lieu of Base Rent.

clauses.heading: Co-Tenancy Termination Right
clauses.text: If the Co-Tenancy Condition remains unsatisfied for a continuous period of two hundred seventy (270) days following Tenant's election to pay Substitute Rent under Section 7.1, Tenant shall have the right to terminate this Lease upon sixty (60) days' prior written notice to Landlord, with neither party having any further liability to the other except for obligations accrued prior to the effective date of termination and Tenant's obligation to surrender the Premises in the condition required by Section 19.

clauses.heading: Insurance
clauses.text: Tenant shall maintain throughout the Term at Tenant's expense: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 aggregate, specifically endorsing Tenant's hydrotherapy, cryotherapy, and recovery suite operations; (b) professional liability (errors and omissions) insurance of not less than $1,000,000 per claim; (c) property insurance at replacement cost covering Tenant's medical-grade equipment and improvements; and (d) workers' compensation as required under Arizona law. All policies shall name Landlord as additional insured. Current certificate on file through September 30, 2026.

documents.title: Retail Lease — Anthropologie Group LLC at Riverside Square

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 6,800 rentable square feet, the sum of $47.75 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $27,058.33 in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the Term, without offset, deduction, or demand. Base Rent shall be subject to the annual escalation set forth in Section 4.2 commencing in Lease Year 2.

clauses.heading: Fixed Annual Escalation
clauses.text: Commencing on the first anniversary of the Commencement Date and on each anniversary thereafter, Base Rent shall increase by three percent (3%) over the Base Rent payable in the immediately preceding Lease Year. Such increases shall be cumulative and compounding and shall not be subject to adjustment based on any index, market condition, or other factor. The escalated rent schedule, calculated from a base of $47.75 per rentable square foot, shall govern regardless of any change in occupancy levels at the Shopping Center.

clauses.heading: Insurance — Tenant's Obligations
clauses.text: Tenant shall at all times during the Term maintain, at Tenant's sole cost, the following: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 in the aggregate; (b) all-risk property insurance on Tenant's merchandise, trade fixtures, and Tenant's Work at full replacement cost; and (c) workers' compensation and employer's liability as required under Georgia law. All policies naming Landlord and any mortgagee as additional insureds. NOTE: Certificate of insurance on file has expired. Tenant has not delivered a renewal certificate as of the date of this review.

documents.title: Second Amendment to Retail Lease — Anthropologie Group LLC

clauses.heading: Extended Term
clauses.text: The Term of the Lease, as previously amended, is hereby extended for a period of two (2) years, such that the new Expiration Date shall be October 31, 2028, unless sooner terminated in accordance with the terms of the Lease. Base Rent during the Extension Period shall continue to escalate at three percent (3%) per annum as provided in Section 4.2 of the Lease, applied to the Base Rent in effect as of November 1, 2026.

clauses.heading: Tenant Improvement Allowance
clauses.text: In consideration of Tenant's agreement to extend the Term as provided herein, Landlord shall provide Tenant with a tenant improvement allowance not to exceed $75,000.00 (the 'TI Allowance'), to be applied toward the cost of Tenant's approved interior renovation of the Premises. The TI Allowance shall be disbursed by Landlord within thirty (30) days following receipt of (i) lien waivers from all contractors and subcontractors, (ii) a certificate of completion from Tenant's architect, and (iii) evidence that all required permits have been finaled. Unused TI Allowance shall be forfeited.

documents.title: Retail Lease — Five Below Inc. at Northgate Plaza

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 8,500 rentable square feet, the sum of $18.50 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $13,104.17 in advance on the first day of each calendar month. Base Rent shall be subject to the annual escalation provisions of Section 4.2. This Lease expires January 31, 2026; Tenant has not delivered a certificate of insurance as required under Section 11.1.

clauses.heading: Annual Rent Escalation
clauses.text: Effective on the first day of Lease Year 2 and each Lease Year thereafter, Base Rent shall increase by two percent (2%) over the Base Rent in effect during the immediately preceding Lease Year. Such increases are cumulative and compounding. For reference, Base Rent in Lease Year 10 (February 1, 2025 – January 31, 2026) shall be $22.57 per rentable square foot per annum ($15,988.38 per month).

clauses.heading: Insurance Requirements
clauses.text: Tenant shall procure and maintain throughout the Term at Tenant's expense: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 aggregate; (b) all-risk property insurance on Tenant's merchandise, trade fixtures, and leasehold improvements at replacement cost; and (c) workers' compensation as required under Minnesota law. Tenant shall name Landlord and any recorded mortgagee as additional insureds and deliver certificates to Landlord no later than ten (10) days prior to Commencement and within ten (10) days of each renewal.

documents.title: First Amendment to Retail Lease — Five Below Inc.

clauses.heading: Parking Expansion — Exclusive Loading Area
clauses.text: Section 14.2 of the Lease is hereby amended to designate two (2) parking spaces immediately adjacent to the Tenant's rear delivery entrance (Spaces P-44 and P-45 as shown on Exhibit A-1 hereto) as exclusive loading and unloading spaces for Tenant's use during Tenant's store hours. Such spaces shall be marked 'Tenant Loading — No Parking' and shall not be used by other tenants or the general public. Landlord shall bear the cost of marking and signage for such spaces.

clauses.heading: HVAC Replacement — Cost Allocation
clauses.text: Landlord and Tenant agree that the cost of replacing the rooftop HVAC unit serving the Premises (Unit RTU-7, approximately 15-ton capacity) shall be shared equally between the parties, with each party contributing fifty percent (50%) of the documented replacement cost, not to exceed $18,000.00 per party. Landlord shall manage the replacement and invoice Tenant for Tenant's share within thirty (30) days of completion. Tenant's contribution shall be payable within thirty (30) days of receipt of Landlord's invoice.

documents.title: Retail Lease — Ulta Beauty Inc. at Elm Street Marketplace

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 5,200 rentable square feet, the sum of $52.00 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $22,533.33 in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the Term. Rent is due without notice or demand, and Tenant waives any right to deduct from or offset against rent amounts claimed to be owed by Landlord. Base Rent is subject to annual increases as set forth in Section 4.2.

clauses.heading: Annual Rent Escalation
clauses.text: On the first day of Lease Year 2 and each Lease Year thereafter, Base Rent shall increase by three percent (3%) over the immediately preceding Lease Year's Base Rent. Increases are cumulative and compounding. The escalated monthly rent obligations for the remainder of the Term, calculated from the Commencement Date at the agreed escalation rate, are incorporated by reference in Exhibit C to this Lease.

clauses.heading: Insurance Obligations
clauses.text: Tenant shall, at Tenant's sole cost and expense, maintain throughout the Term: (a) commercial general liability insurance with per-occurrence limits of $2,000,000 and annual aggregate limits of $4,000,000, covering all operations in and from the Premises including beauty services and retail sales; (b) product liability coverage of not less than $2,000,000 per claim; (c) all-risk property insurance at replacement cost covering Tenant's trade fixtures, salon equipment, and inventory; and (d) workers' compensation as required under Massachusetts law. Landlord, Landlord's managing agent, and any holder of a mortgage on the Property shall be named additional insureds.

property.name: Sunset Crossing
property.address: 4515 La Jolla Village Drive, San Diego, CA 92122

documents.title: Retail Lease — Sephora USA Inc. at Sunset Crossing

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 3,100 rentable square feet, the sum of $58.25 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $15,047.92 in advance on the first day of each calendar month. Base Rent shall be subject to annual CPI adjustment as provided in Section 4.2 commencing on the first anniversary of the Rent Commencement Date.

clauses.heading: CPI Adjustment
clauses.text: Base Rent shall be adjusted annually on each anniversary of the Rent Commencement Date in an amount equal to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers, San Diego-Carlsbad metropolitan area, published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, over the twelve (12) month period ending sixty (60) days prior to the applicable adjustment date. No annual CPI adjustment shall exceed four percent (4%) or be less than one percent (1%). Adjusted rent shall be confirmed in writing by Landlord within thirty (30) days of each adjustment date.

clauses.heading: Insurance
clauses.text: Tenant shall procure and continuously maintain at Tenant's expense: (a) commercial general liability insurance, with per-occurrence limits of $2,000,000 and aggregate limits of $4,000,000; (b) product liability insurance of not less than $3,000,000 per claim, given Tenant's cosmetics retail and in-store application services; (c) all-risk property insurance at replacement cost covering Tenant's trade fixtures, merchandise, and leasehold improvements; and (d) workers' compensation as required under California law. All policies shall name Landlord, Landlord's property manager, and Landlord's lender as additional insureds. Certificates shall be delivered to Landlord no later than the Commencement Date and on each renewal date.

documents.title: Retail Lease — Orangetheory Fitness Inc. at Gateway Center

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 3,800 rentable square feet, the sum of $24.50 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $7,758.33 in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the Term. Base Rent is subject to the escalation schedule set forth in Section 4.2.

clauses.heading: Annual Escalation
clauses.text: Effective on the first day of each Lease Year following Lease Year 1, Base Rent shall increase by two and one-half percent (2.5%) over the Base Rent payable in the immediately preceding Lease Year. Such escalations are cumulative and compounding and shall occur automatically without further notice. The parties acknowledge that Base Rent in Lease Year 8 (the final Lease Year) shall be approximately $29.44 per rentable square foot per annum.

clauses.heading: Required Insurance Coverages
clauses.text: Tenant shall maintain throughout the Term, at Tenant's sole expense: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 aggregate, specifically endorsed for fitness center and group exercise operations including participant injury; (b) property insurance at replacement cost covering Tenant's cardio and rowing equipment and leasehold improvements; and (c) workers' compensation and employer's liability as required by Texas law. Landlord, Landlord's property manager, and Landlord's lender shall be named additional insureds.

documents.title: Reciprocal Easement Agreement — Gateway Center

clauses.heading: Shared Parking and Access
clauses.text: Owner of Parcel A (the anchor parcel) hereby grants Owner of Parcel B (the in-line retail parcel) and their respective tenants, customers, and invitees a perpetual, non-exclusive easement for access, ingress, egress, and parking over the Common Parking Area as delineated on Exhibit A-2 hereto. This easement shall run with the land and be binding upon all successors and assigns of each parcel owner. Parking shall be shared on an unreserved, first-come, first-served basis, except for spaces designated for handicapped use pursuant to applicable law.

clauses.heading: Common Area Maintenance Cost Sharing
clauses.text: All costs of maintaining, repairing, and replacing Common Area improvements (including parking lot resurfacing, exterior lighting, and landscaping) that benefit both parcels shall be allocated fifty-five percent (55%) to the anchor parcel and forty-five percent (45%) to the in-line retail parcel, based on relative gross leasable area. Each party shall maintain a commercially reasonable maintenance program consistent with a first-class retail shopping center in the Dallas-Fort Worth metropolitan area. Annual reconciliation statements shall be provided within sixty (60) days following the close of each calendar year.

clauses.heading: Pylon and Monument Signage
clauses.text: Pylon signage at the primary ingress points to the Shopping Center shall be maintained by the anchor parcel owner at anchor parcel owner's expense. In-line retail tenants occupying more than 3,000 rentable square feet shall be entitled to one (1) panel position on each pylon sign serving the in-line retail building, subject to applicable zoning and sign code restrictions. Tenant panel positions shall be allocated by size, with larger tenants receiving priority, and shall be subject to the Landlord's signage criteria in effect from time to time.

documents.title: Medical Office Lease — Pacific Dental Services LLC at Summit Village

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 2,800 rentable square feet configured for dental office use, the sum of $32.00 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $7,466.67 in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the Term. Base Rent is subject to annual escalation as set forth in Section 4.2. This Lease expires March 31, 2026; the current certificate of insurance expires February 28, 2026 and has not been renewed.

clauses.heading: Rent Escalation
clauses.text: Effective on the first anniversary of the Commencement Date and each anniversary thereafter, Base Rent shall increase by two percent (2%) over the Base Rent in effect for the immediately preceding Lease Year. Such increases are cumulative and compounding. Base Rent in the final Lease Year (April 1, 2025 – March 31, 2026) shall be $37.85 per rentable square foot per annum ($8,830.00 per month), reflecting nine (9) annual escalations from the initial rate of $32.00.

clauses.heading: Insurance — Medical Office Use
clauses.text: Tenant shall, at Tenant's sole cost and expense, maintain throughout the Term: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 aggregate; (b) professional liability (malpractice) insurance for all dental practitioners operating in the Premises with limits of not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $3,000,000 aggregate; (c) property insurance at replacement cost covering dental equipment, cabinetry, and Tenant's leasehold improvements; and (d) workers' compensation as required under Oregon law. All liability policies shall name Landlord as additional insured.

documents.title: Retail Lease — H&R Block Inc. at Orchard Park Center

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 1,800 rentable square feet, the sum of $22.00 per rentable square foot per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $3,300.00 in advance on the first day of each calendar month during the Term. Payment shall be made without offset, deduction, or prior demand. Base Rent shall be subject to annual increases as provided in Section 4.2.

clauses.heading: Annual Escalation
clauses.text: Effective January 1 of each calendar year following the first full calendar year of the Term, Base Rent shall increase by one and one-half percent (1.5%) over the Base Rent in effect during the immediately preceding calendar year. Such increases shall be cumulative. The parties acknowledge that the relatively modest escalation rate reflects Tenant's seasonal operational model, with peak occupancy intensity during the January–April tax filing season.

clauses.heading: Insurance
clauses.text: Tenant shall maintain at Tenant's cost throughout the Term: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 aggregate; (b) errors and omissions (professional liability) insurance of not less than $1,000,000 per claim covering tax preparation services; (c) property insurance at replacement cost for Tenant's equipment and leasehold improvements; and (d) workers' compensation as required under Ohio law. All policies shall name Landlord as additional insured. Certificates shall be renewed annually and delivered to Landlord no later than January 1 of each year.

documents.title: First Amendment to Retail Lease — H&R Block Inc.

clauses.heading: Permitted Hours of Operation
clauses.text: Section 7.2 of the Lease is hereby amended to permit Tenant to operate extended hours during the period January 15 through April 18 of each calendar year (the 'Tax Season Period'). During the Tax Season Period, Tenant may operate the Premises from 8:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m., Monday through Saturday, and 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. on Sundays. Standard operating hours per the original Lease shall apply at all other times during the Term.

clauses.heading: Exterior Signage — Seasonal Campaign
clauses.text: Notwithstanding Section 13.1 of the Lease, Tenant is hereby permitted to install temporary window graphics and exterior banners not exceeding forty (40) square feet in total area during the Tax Season Period, provided such signage complies with applicable municipal sign codes and Landlord's signage criteria. All temporary signage shall be removed by April 30 of each calendar year. Permanent signage changes shall continue to require Landlord's prior written approval as provided in the Lease.

documents.title: Retail Lease — European Wax Center Inc. at Bayshore Commons

clauses.heading: Base Rent
clauses.text: Tenant shall pay to Landlord, as Base Rent for the Premises consisting of approximately 1,400 rentable square feet, the sums per rentable square foot set forth in the stepped rent schedule in Section 4.2, payable in equal monthly installments in advance on the first day of each calendar month, without offset or deduction.

clauses.heading: Stepped Rent Schedule
clauses.text: Base Rent shall be payable during the following periods at the rates set forth below: (i) Lease Years 1 through 4 (September 1, 2021 – August 31, 2025): $25.00 per rentable square foot per annum ($2,916.67 per month); (ii) Lease Years 5 through 8 (September 1, 2025 – August 31, 2029): $28.50 per rentable square foot per annum ($3,325.00 per month). Each rent step shall be self-effectuating without further notice or amendment.

clauses.heading: Insurance
clauses.text: Tenant shall maintain throughout the Term at Tenant's cost: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of $2,000,000 per occurrence and $4,000,000 aggregate, specifically covering waxing and personal care service operations; (b) professional liability insurance of not less than $1,000,000 per claim; (c) property insurance at replacement cost covering Tenant's waxing equipment, retail product inventory, and leasehold improvements; and (d) workers' compensation as required under Florida law. Landlord and any mortgagee shall be named as additional insureds on all liability policies.

clauses.heading: Renewal Option
clauses.text: Provided Tenant is not in default beyond applicable notice and cure periods, Tenant shall have one (1) option to renew this Lease for one additional five (5) year period, exercisable by written notice delivered to Landlord no later than nine (9) months prior to the expiration of the initial Term. Base Rent during the Renewal Term shall be the then-prevailing fair market rental rate for comparable personal-care retail space in the North Tampa submarket, but in no event less than the Base Rent in effect in the final Lease Year of the initial Term.
